claim: Verification confirms that gathered evidence is sufficient and properly structured for PRD readiness. No assumptions are presented as facts, all evidence paths are usable and verified, and missing data is properly reflected in assumptions.

evidence.summary: Verified: Core dispatch implementation exists and is correctly cited in both evidence results with identical line ranges.
evidence.why: Confirms evidence accuracy and file path validity.

evidence.summary: Verified: Provider isolation logic exists and is accurately described across both evidence results.
evidence.why: Confirms provider implementation evidence is factual.

evidence.summary: Verified: Boundary enforcement test suite exists with comprehensive coverage of mutation detection.
evidence.why: Confirms test evidence supports boundary enforcement claims.

evidence.summary: Verified: PRD readiness inspection logic exists and validates evidence scope declarations.

assumptions: Git-based boundary detection works correctly in the target project repository
Provider CLI commands (claude, codex) are available and correctly versioned in the execution environment
risks: If git-based boundary detection fails, evidence agents could silently mutate project files
If provider CLI commands are unavailable, evidence dispatch will fail at Runtime
A bug in boundary snapshot logic could fail to detect project mutations

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker results are properly aligned with no conflicts. All evidence records use valid project-scoped sources with accurate file paths and line numbers. Assumptions are correctly separated from facts and reflect Runtime environmental factors. Missing data is empty. Evidence is sufficient for PRD readiness.
